reviews.review_msg: Amazing guy, great sense of humor and easy to understand lectures. By far the most interesting course I've taken at SFU. He's very humble and open to questions. Definitely recommend. Exams were super easy, as long as you get basics, sometimes you can make things up (logically) and do well. I crammed for the entire course and got an A. :)

Pretty good at teaching, funny, and uses lots of real-life examples. Exams are fair but they tend to test specifically and sometimes randomly so you need to remember everything (lots of slides, all written). Hard to know what's important. Simultaneously the class I learned and suffered the most in so I don't recommend taking this lol

Weekly quizzes make up 50% of grade. Research Paper requires effort and consult him for guidance! Final was just like the quizzes but focus on big picture ideas! Hard class but it's scaled nicely, everyone thinks they aren't going to do well, for example I was at around 75% at the end of the course but got an A. Just keep this in mind (sleep easy).

Quizzes out of 16 marks every other week, all quizzes worth 60% together with Final. Didn't have Midterm, which was great. Final is one big quiz out of 40 marks. Has a sarcastic sense of humor which I thought was not inappropriate in class. Educates you rather than schooling you. Even if you didn't do well on the tests, you will learn lots.

Crespi is very knowledgeable, but unfortunately comes across as condescending and negatively sarcastic towards the class. The exams test very broadly while his classes are extremely detailed. He marks harshly "to motivate" the class to work harder.
